description_en: Ackermannschule is a public primary school located in the vibrant Gallus neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. Positioned at Ackermannstraße 37, it provides foundational education for children aged approximately 6 to 10 years. With an enrollment of 293 students, the school offers a personalized learning environment where core subjects such as German, mathematics, and general studies are taught in accordance with Hesse's state curriculum standards. Ackermannschule emphasizes play-based learning, social integration, and essential literacy and numeracy skills. The multicultural fabric of Gallus enriches the school community, promoting inclusivity and support for children from diverse backgrounds. Parents appreciate the school’s central location, making drop-off and pick-up convenient, supported by Frankfurt’s efficient public transport system. As a publicly funded institution, there are no tuition fees, and resources are allocated for extracurricular activities and school meals through municipal budgets. For families residing in Gallus, Ackermannschule is a dependable choice for quality primary education, laying a solid foundation for children's future success.

description: Adolf-Reichwein-Schule ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Lenzenbergstraße 70, 65931 Frankfurt am Main. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en:
The Adolf-Reichwein-Schule is a public primary school located in the southern part of Frankfurt am Main, serving families since its establishment in 1961. As an open all-day school, it provides a comprehensive care program that extends beyond traditional school hours, accommodating working parents in a supportive environment. With an enrollment of approximately 290 students, the school fosters a nurturing learning atmosphere where individualized attention and strong relationships between teachers, students, and parents are highly valued.

A standout feature of the Adolf-Reichwein-Schule is its integrated Schulkindergarten, which helps younger children transition smoothly into primary education. The school’s approach emphasizes social skills, creative expression, and physical activity through a varied afternoon program that includes play, homework assistance, and extracurricular activities. Guided by the values of openness, inclusivity, and democratic education, inspired by its namesake, educator Adolf Reichwein, the school prepares children not only academically but also for collaborative life in Frankfurt's diverse urban environment. Parents appreciate this holistic approach that nurtures young learners. For the latest information on open houses or enrollment procedures, families are encouraged to directly contact the school administration.

description_en: Aktive Schule Frankfurt is a private alternative school located in Frankfurt am Main, offering integrated education from kindergarten through primary school and into a comprehensive secondary school. Established in 2004, the school emphasizes holistic child development, nurturing children's natural joy for life, curiosity, self-confidence, individuality, and respect for others. Situated in the Frankfurter Berg neighborhood on a spacious 6,000 square meter campus designed specifically for its educational philosophy, the school follows a pedagogical model inspired by free alternative schools. Children learn in mixed-age groups, allowing them to explore their curiosity and engage in self-directed learning. Daily classes run from 8:30 a.m. to 1:00 p.m., with parent-provided care available until 2:30 p.m. on four days a week. The Aktive Schule Frankfurt offers a seamless educational pathway without traditional grade transitions and prioritizes sustainability in its modern facilities. Here, children thrive in a supportive environment that encourages autonomy and community. Interested families are invited to attend information evenings to learn more about this unique and joyful learning approach.

tuition_granular_reasoning: According to the official website, as of August 2024, Aktive Schule Frankfurt explicitly avoids an income-based sliding scale ('keine einkommensabhängige Staffelung'). Instead, the school charges a standard flat fee of €280 per month. Families facing financial hardship can request a reduction; Bürgergeld recipients and Frankfurt Pass holders pay exactly half the standard rate (€140), which is modeled for the lowest income bracket. Other lower-income households (21-30k) may receive a partial reduction, estimated at €220 based on historical minimums. All other income brackets pay the standard flat fee of €280. The school offers sibling discounts ('Geschwisterermäßigung'), but since exact current percentages are not publicly specified, conservative defaults of 10%, 15%, and 20% are applied.
tuition_tier_reasoning: According to the official school website, the standard monthly tuition fee for the primary school (Grundschule) is €280 as of August 2024. While reductions are possible upon application, the regular monthly fee of €280 places the school in the 'medium' cost tier (€101-300/month).

description_en: The Albert-Schweitzer-Schule is a public primary school located in the Frankfurter Berg area, providing inviting educational opportunities for local families. With a student population of 275, the school fosters a vibrant community for children aged 6 to 10 across four grade levels (Classes 1-4). It is notably recognized for its Ganztagsprofil 2 status, which indicates a full-day operation that promotes flexible learning environments. To support working parents, the school offers care services from 7:30 AM to 3:00 PM, along with extended care until 5:00 PM. Unique programs such as a preparatory class for preschoolers and an intensive class for targeted support set the Albert-Schweitzer-Schule apart. Additionally, English language instruction begins in grade 3, providing early exposure to the language. The school features a variety of extracurricular activities, including class trips and environmental projects that encourage teamwork and social skills. With its open-door policy and integration into the local community, the school serves as a nurturing environment where children can thrive.

The school opens its doors at 7:30 a.m., providing early supervision where children can settle in and prepare for the school day. With structured lessons from 8:00 a.m. to 12:35 p.m., including breaks for outdoor play or quiet activities, students enjoy a balanced daily routine. A standout feature is the school's engagement in the 'Pact for All-Day Schooling', offering reliable after-school care until 5:00 p.m. This school emphasizes strong parent involvement and integrates local initiatives into its curriculum. With approximately 364 students and a dedicated teaching staff, Albrecht-Dürer-Schule fosters a personal and supportive learning atmosphere. The school is committed to providing a solid foundation for education that nurtures both academic and social skills.

description_en: The Alexander-Puschkin-Schule is a bilingual and bicultural primary school in Frankfurt am Main, providing high-quality education for grades 1 through 4 within a private, state-approved framework. Founded in the 2013/2014 school year, it is the only German-Russian all-day primary school in the city, welcoming children who speak German or Russian as their first or heritage language. The school emphasizes a language-intensive environment where German and Russian children learn together in an atmosphere of empathy and cultural exchange. By the end of grade 4, students achieve native-like proficiency in German, while Russian is taught both as a mother tongue and a foreign language. Additionally, English is incorporated into afternoon programs to broaden students' linguistic horizons. The school places a strong focus on STEM subjects and offers a variety of activities, including holiday programs and elective activities tailored to working families. With small class sizes and a family-like atmosphere, the Alexander-Puschkin-Schule provides individualized support and fosters the holistic development of children. Renowned for its vibrant community and dedicated teachers, the school creates a positive learning environment where students thrive.

tuition_granular_reasoning: [GPT-5.2 Pass3] Official fee table on the school’s website (Kosten/Стоимость обучения) for Schuljahr 2026/2027 (dated Stand: 17.03.2026) lists flat monthly Schulgeld amounts per child (not income-based): 445€/month for the 1st child, 380€/month for the 2nd child, 230€/month for the 3rd child. Therefore, for the requested household gross annual income points, the computed monthly tuition (Schulgeld for one child) is the same (445€) for every income bracket. The website does not define any income measure (no household-gross/worldwide-income rule) because tuition is not income-tiered. The 2026/27 table does not explicitly state the number of billing months; an older 2025/26 version of the same cost page stated the monthly fees are calculated on the basis of a school year from 1 Aug to 31 Jul and are payable monthly even during holidays by the 3rd working day—suggesting 12 monthly payments.
tuition_tier_reasoning: According to the official fee table for the 2026/2027 school year, the Alexander-Puschkin-Schule charges a standard monthly tuition (Schulgeld) of 445 EUR for the first child in primary school (Grades 1-4). This base fee covers classes up to 13:30 or 14:20 and includes breakfast and lunch. Optional afternoon care (Nachmittagsbetreuung) is an additional 245 EUR per month. Excluding the separate aftercare fee, the base monthly tuition of 445 EUR falls squarely into the 'high' cost tier (€301-500/month).

description_en: The Anna-Schmidt-Schule is a state-recognized private elementary school located in the vibrant Westend district of Frankfurt am Main, serving approximately 375 students across 15 classes in the 2024/25 academic year. The school offers both a Montessori-inspired program and a traditional elementary school track, providing parents with flexible educational options. With a history dating back to 1886, the school emphasizes individualized learning aligned with Hesse's state curriculum while integrating modern digital teaching methods to foster a love for learning. Its full-day operation, running from 8:00 am to 2:00 pm, includes after-school activities, homework assistance, and a hot lunch provided in the on-site cafeteria. As a UNESCO Project School since 1979, it places great importance on international perspectives and cultural exchange. The school focuses on STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) education, promoting creativity and critical thinking through hands-on projects. Located in a dynamic community, the school offers modern facilities and a supportive environment that ensures a seamless transition to higher grades. The Anna-Schmidt-Schule stands out by blending Montessori freedom with structured learning, equipping children with the confidence and competence needed for a connected world.

description_en: The Astrid-Lindgren-Schule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, catering to students from first to fourth grade in a supportive and participatory learning environment. Named after the renowned Swedish children's author Astrid Lindgren, the school draws inspiration from her values, emphasizing creativity, empathy, and a child-centered approach to education. With approximately 335 students, the school reflects a stable community presence in its urban neighborhood, offering families a valuable educational institution. The school prioritizes transparent communication, assisting families in preparing for the transition to secondary school through helpful resources such as presentations and informational flyers. At Astrid-Lindgren-Schule, respectful collaboration is encouraged, allowing children to grow both academically and socially. The school is easily accessible and integrates seamlessly into Frankfurt's diverse educational landscape. Overall, Astrid-Lindgren-Schule provides a reliable foundation for early education, making it an ideal choice for parents seeking a public primary school that combines literary inspiration with a welcoming learning environment.

description_en: The August-Gräser-Schule is a public primary school located in the Schwanheim district of Frankfurt am Main, accommodating over 250 students. Situated in a historic building that has been educating local children since 1903, the school emphasizes an inclusive and creative learning environment. It was awarded the golden seal as an Internet-ABC School in 2018, highlighting its commitment to safe and skilled internet use. Furthermore, in 2025, it became certified as a KulturSchule (Cultural School), reflecting its dedication to cultural education. The curriculum promotes modern teaching methods that foster teamwork, social responsibility, and digital literacy. English is introduced as a foreign language starting in grade 3, with French available as an elective. The school focuses on individualized support and offers a structured, family-oriented daily schedule from 8:30 a.m. to 1:45 p.m. Its extended care program encourages creative activities and social interactions in a safe environment. Led by principal Saskia Ghribi, the August-Gräser-Schule is a vibrant community that celebrates diversity, culture, and inclusion.

description_en: Berkersheimer Grundschule is a public primary school located in the Berkersheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, catering to young students aged 6 to 10. Positioned at Berkersheimer Untergasse 18, the school is easily accessible, making it a convenient choice for local families. With an enrollment of 274 students for the 2024/25 school year, it offers a stable community presence and a nurturing environment that promotes social development. description_en: The Berthold-Otto-Schule is a public primary school located in the Griesheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, providing a welcoming atmosphere for approximately 290 to 313 students from grades 1 to 4. The school employs innovative, child-centered teaching methods inspired by the pedagogy of its namesake, Berthold Otto, promoting holistic, experience-based education. With mixed-age classes in grades 1 and 2 as well as 3 and 4, students benefit from strong, lasting relationships with their teachers. A notable feature of the school is its comprehensive swimming program for grades 3 and 4, which helps many students achieve the silver swimming badge. Music is also a significant part of the curriculum, with annual school trips and performances for parents. The school prioritizes digital learning and offers extensive all-day care with a variety of extracurricular activities. Through strong community collaboration and an upcoming expansion to a new campus in Griesheim, the Berthold-Otto-Schule stands out as a modern and inclusive educational choice for families.

description_en:
Boehleschule is a public primary school located at Jungmannstraße 3-5 in the Griesheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. It serves as a welcoming educational hub for young learners, with a current enrollment of 251 students and projections for growth to approximately 290 students by the 2025/26 school year. The school is committed to inclusive education and equal opportunities, proudly participating in the federal Startchancen program, which supports equitable educational chances for all students, particularly those from diverse backgrounds.

In addition to standard classes, Boehleschule offers two preparatory courses and one pre-class to help ease the transition into formal education. The curriculum is enriched with various extracurricular clubs that allow students to explore their interests in the arts, sports, and music. The school also engages in the Erasmus+ project, fostering international collaboration with partner institutions across Europe, focusing on themes of diversity and identity. Boehleschule features a full-day program that extends learning and care options, including a warm communal lunch and supervised homework assistance. Families will find Boehleschule an appealing choice for a nurturing and holistic educational environment that emphasizes growth and community involvement.

description_en: Bonifatiusschule, located in the vibrant Bockenheim district of Frankfurt am Main, is a public primary school dedicated to nurturing the individual potential of its 248 students. The school's educational philosophy emphasizes modern teaching methods that foster curiosity and creativity while ensuring a solid foundation in core subjects such as German, mathematics, and natural sciences. Bonifatiusschule prides itself on creating an inclusive learning environment where children from diverse backgrounds can learn and grow together. In addition to the regular curriculum, the school offers a variety of extracurricular activities designed to enhance students' social and creative skills. These include sports, arts, and theater projects that occur regularly, fostering a strong sense of community within the school. The dedicated teaching staff works closely with parents, organizing regular events and informational evenings to maintain open communication and collaboration. Bonifatiusschule is an ideal place for children to develop a strong educational foundation while also acquiring essential social competencies.

description_en: Brentanoschule is a small public primary school located in the Rödelheim district of Frankfurt am Main. Catering to approximately 217 students across 10 regular classes and 2 intensive support classes, the school fosters a close-knit atmosphere where individual attention is prioritized. Under the leadership of Principal Konstanze Kutschera, the dedicated team of educators ensures that children receive comprehensive support for both academic and social development. Nestled near green spaces and the Nidda River, the school offers ample opportunities for environmental exploration, encouraging a connection with nature right from the playground. A standout feature of Brentanoschule is its commitment to inclusivity, providing a fully accessible campus that allows all children to participate comfortably. The school’s well-designed outdoor areas include diverse play zones that promote active recreation. Additionally, music is integrated into the curriculum to enhance creativity and engagement among students. Brentanoschule hosts various events and provides resources for parents, fostering a supportive school community. This makes it an ideal choice for families seeking a warm and inclusive educational environment for their children.

description_en: Comeniusschule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, renowned for its committed and supportive learning environment. Catering to children aged 6 to 10, it encompasses grades 1 through 4. The school emphasizes the development of fundamental skills in reading, writing, mathematics, and social studies, alongside arts, physical education, and basic sciences. With approximately 370 students, Comeniusschule strikes a pleasant balance between individualized attention and a vibrant community where friendships can flourish. The school is entirely tuition-free, ensuring accessibility for all families, and often provides additional support services like school meals and after-school care. Comeniusschule is a place where children feel comfortable and are encouraged to reach their full potential. Regular events such as school festivals and sports days foster family involvement, helping integrate families into the Frankfurt community.

description_en: Dahlmannschule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, providing a solid educational foundation for young students in the local community. With 367 students enrolled for the 2024/25 school year, the school offers a structured environment tailored to the developmental needs of children in grades 1 through 4. Committed to the Hesse state curriculum, Dahlmannschule emphasizes core subjects such as German, mathematics, and general knowledge, while also fostering social skills, creativity, and independent learning. The school provides practical resources for parents to support home learning, including clear guidelines for homework, ensuring that children reinforce classroom lessons without introducing new content prematurely. Its location at Luxemburgerallee 24 is convenient for local families and easily accessible. As a public institution, Dahlmannschule promotes an inclusive and supportive atmosphere where children can build friendships and develop routines. This school is an excellent choice for families seeking accessible and quality primary education.

With approximately 369 students enrolled for the 2024/25 school year, this mid-sized institution fosters a close-knit learning environment where teachers can offer personalized attention. Core subjects are taught primarily in German, with opportunities for introductory exposure to English or other languages. Diesterwegschule is also involved in city-wide initiatives such as environmental education and sports events, promoting inclusivity among its diverse student body. This school exemplifies high educational standards, providing a stable, community-oriented setting ideal for building strong foundations. Families are encouraged to reach out to the school office for enrollment information.

description_en:
Ebelfeldschule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, nestled in the residential neighborhood of Praunheim. With 381 enrolled students, the school fosters a warm and family-oriented environment for children aged 6 to 10. It emphasizes individualized attention through manageable class sizes, allowing for personalized support from teachers. The curriculum aligns with Hesse's educational standards and focuses on core subjects such as German, math, and general knowledge.

A key strength of Ebelfeldschule is its active parental involvement. Traditional events, such as the welcoming ceremony for new first-graders on August 28, 2024, strengthen the connection between home and school. Regular parent evenings are held to discuss children's progress and provide support strategies, creating a collaborative community.

The school also offers a range of extracurricular activities that promote holistic child development, including sports and creative projects. Its convenient location, with good public transport links, makes it an accessible choice for local families. Overall, Ebelfeldschule plays an essential role in Frankfurt's educational landscape, providing students with a solid foundation for their academic journey.

description: Eichendorffschule ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Waldschulstraße 83, 65933 Frankfurt am Main. 4% der Schüler haben nichtdeutsche Herkunftssprache. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en:
Eichendorffschule is a public primary school located in the Griesheim district of Frankfurt am Main, serving children from grades 1 to 4. With an enrollment of 290 students, the school provides a stable environment in this family-friendly neighborhood. As an all-day school, Eichendorffschule emphasizes a balanced daily schedule that integrates structured learning with physical activity. Through innovative teaching methods such as open lessons, project-based work, and seasonal projects, the school fosters holistic development in children. 

Additionally, the school offers extended care through the 'Erweiterte Schulische Betreuung' (ESB) program, in partnership with the Internationaler Bund, available daily from 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. for children aged 6 to 10. This program includes lunch, homework assistance, and leisure activities in a supportive environment. A special focus on literacy is evident through a well-equipped school library and the 'Sternpiloten' program, which aids in emotional and educational growth. 

Eichendorffschule not only promotes academic achievement but also social skills and personal development. The school's collaboration with local partners contributes to a vibrant, community-oriented atmosphere. These features make Eichendorffschule an excellent choice for families seeking a high-quality, tuition-free primary education in Frankfurt.

description: Elsa-Brändström-Schule ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Lindenstraße 2, 60325 Frankfurt am Main. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en:
The Elsa-Brändström-Schule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, focusing on foundational education for young students. Enrolling 271 students for the 2024/25 school year, the school provides tuition-free education and adheres to Hesse's state educational standards. Named after the humanitarian figure Elsa Brändström, known for her efforts during World War I, the school embodies values of compassion and community support. It serves children typically aged 6 to 10, covering grades 1 through 4, with an emphasis on core subjects such as German, mathematics, and social development.

One of the school's standout features is its commitment to education for sustainable development. Recently recognized by the Hessian Ministry for the Environment, the Elsa-Brändström-Schule was awarded the title of "School Year of Sustainability," highlighting its integration of sustainability into everyday school life. This recognition showcases the entire school community's active participation in environmentally friendly practices, fostering responsible citizenship among students. The school provides a nurturing environment that balances individualized attention with communal activities. Families considering enrollment are encouraged to contact the school or Frankfurt's public school administration to learn more about daily life, extracurricular offerings, and the admission process.

description_en: The Emma-Kann-Grundschule is a modern public primary school located in the vibrant Europaviertel neighborhood of the Gallus district in Frankfurt am Main. Opened in September 2025, the school accommodates up to five classes per grade, creating a welcoming environment for families. The new building integrates a primary school, all-day care, and an on-site kindergarten, addressing the growing needs of the community. A standout feature is the adjacent sports hall, which is beautifully integrated into the landscape, offering views of the nearby Gleisfeldpark. The school emphasizes an open and interactive architecture that fosters learning in bright, inviting spaces. With a commitment to being an open-minded place of learning and living, it promotes respect and tolerance, providing numerous resources for parents to facilitate transitions to secondary education. Under the leadership of Principal Soo-Young Hirsch-Yun, the Emma-Kann-Grundschule stands out as a forward-thinking educational hub that blends modern learning with holistic child development.

description_en: The Engelbert-Humperdinck-Schule is a public primary school located in the Westend district of Frankfurt am Main. Named after the renowned German composer Engelbert Humperdinck, the school reflects Germany's rich cultural heritage. Currently serving approximately 315 students in the 2024/25 academic year, it provides a comprehensive foundational education for elementary-aged children. The curriculum includes core subjects such as German language, mathematics, natural sciences, social studies, physical education, and arts. Situated in a vibrant, family-friendly neighborhood, the Engelbert-Humperdinck-Schule is an ideal educational environment for young families. Parents seeking more information about the school are encouraged to contact the institution directly or refer to official educational resources from the state of Hesse.

description: Erasmus Frankfurter Stadtschule (Grundschule) ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Sonnemannstraße 9-11, 60314 Frankfurt am Main. Die Schule ist in privater Trägerschaft. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en: The Erasmus Frankfurter Stadtschule is a private primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, offering a unique trilingual and reform-pedagogical concept. The school emphasizes child-centered learning, fostering creativity and holistic development. Students are immersed in three languages from the outset, enhancing their language skills and cultural awareness in a playful environment. Its all-day structure supports working parents by providing comprehensive care and learning opportunities beyond regular school hours. This includes both structured lessons in the morning and creative afternoon activities such as project work, arts, and sports. Situated in the Ostend district of Frankfurt, the school benefits from its accessible location near public transport and green spaces that promote outdoor learning. With approximately 354 enrolled students, Erasmus Frankfurter Stadtschule offers a personal learning environment that prioritizes individualized instruction and strong teacher-student relationships. Parents appreciate the school's commitment to a balanced and inspiring education that goes beyond academics. The school's warm invitation to families to explore its offerings underscores its inclusive community. Prospective families can easily contact the school at +49 69 90743640 for inquiries.

tuition_granular_reasoning: [GPT-5.2 Pass3] Used Erasmus Frankfurter Stadtschule Grundschule official fee overview ('Kostenstruktur') which states a flat monthly Schulgeld of 566€ for Schuljahr 2025/26 and does not reference household income; therefore tuition is not income-based and the same amount applies to all provided income points. No quantified sibling discount is published on the official fee page; set sibling discount percentages to 0 (not specified).
tuition_tier_reasoning: According to the school's fee structure for the primary school (Grundschule), the monthly tuition is €566. This figure specifically covers tuition and standard afternoon supervision until 16:30, explicitly excluding extra costs for meals (€146/month), optional late-afternoon supervision (€115/month), and annual material fees. A monthly tuition of €566 places the school in the premium cost tier (€501-€750/month).

description_en: The Erich Kästner Schule is a public primary school located in the heart of Frankfurt am Main, catering to approximately 460 students aged 6 to 10. Named after the renowned German author Erich Kästner, famous for his children's books, the school emphasizes a supportive and stimulating learning environment where children can discover and develop their individual talents. Following the Hessian curriculum, the school focuses on foundational skills in reading, writing, mathematics, and social studies, alongside arts, music, sports, and environmental education. Notably, it has been awarded the "Internet ABC Schule 2020" seal, recognizing its commitment to digital safety and media literacy. The Erich Kästner Schule also promotes personal development through various extracurricular activities, including school outings and creative projects. With small class sizes, teachers can pay close attention to each student's needs. For families considering enrollment, admission is typically prioritized based on residency, making the school a vital educational hub in the community.

description_en: The Europäische Schule Frankfurt (Primary School) is a high-quality educational institution that offers education from nursery through primary levels in a multilingual and multicultural environment. Located in the Niederursel neighborhood, the school serves approximately 1,600 students and emphasizes holistic child development, encompassing physical, psychological, social, emotional, and creative aspects. Instruction primarily occurs in the student's language section (German, English, French, or others), focusing on mother tongue, mathematics, and a first foreign language. Additionally, subjects such as art, music, sports, and Discovery of the World are part of the curriculum. A unique feature is the European Hours, which bring together students from different nationalities to foster intercultural understanding. The school provides full-day options and midday meals, supporting working parents with a structured daily routine. A vibrant school life is showcased through events like the colorful Carnival celebration, highlighting creativity and school spirit. The transition to secondary school is thoughtfully managed, ensuring students are well-prepared for their next educational steps. The Europäische Schule Frankfurt is an ideal choice for internationally minded families, nurturing globally aware students in a supportive, linguistically rich setting.

description_en: Fintosch Mehrsprachige Grundschule is a state-approved, non-profit private primary school located in the Westend of Frankfurt. Catering to children from age five, the school offers a unique bilingual education program that combines German and English instruction. Since its opening in the summer of 2017, Fintosch has embraced a philosophy of holistic early education, creating a family-like atmosphere where parents are valued partners in their children's development. With intimate class sizes of up to 20 students, the school ensures personalized attention in a multicultural environment. The curriculum integrates the Hessian state standards with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC), facilitating smooth transitions to either German secondary schools or international pathways after fourth grade. Bilingualism is at the core of the program, with a 50% split in instruction between German and English, allowing children to adapt playfully to school life. Starting in third grade, a third language, such as French, is introduced, enriching the multilingual experience. Fintosch also offers full-day care, extracurricular activities, and a focus on health, all within modern, bright classrooms. The dedicated team of educators, including native speakers, fosters a motivating and creative learning environment. As the only school in Frankfurt that combines Hessian standards with IPC in a bilingual framework, Fintosch provides an ideal bridge for families seeking both local and global education.

description_en: Franckeschule is a public primary school located in the Bockenheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, catering to children in grades 1 through 4. As a "Musical Primary School," it places a strong emphasis on music education, integrating musical activities into daily routines to enhance students' creative development. Additionally, the school is certified as a "School for Climate Protection," promoting environmental awareness and sustainable practices throughout the curriculum. The modern facilities, recently renovated, include a cafeteria, a student library, and outdoor play areas, all designed to foster a supportive learning and social environment. Instruction is primarily in German, with English introduced as an early foreign language to help build bilingual skills from an early age. The school offers a variety of programs, including early childhood education, homework support, and regular events to engage parents actively. Under the leadership of Sabine Ibold and with approximately 389 students, Franckeschule creates a welcoming environment that combines academic rigor with creative expression. Prospective parents are encouraged to attend trial days or contact the school for personalized tours.

description_en: Frankfurt International School (FIS) is a prestigious, independent coeducational day school offering an English-language education based on the International Baccalaureate (IB) framework. The FIS Primary School serves students aged 3 through Grade 5 and emphasizes holistic child development through play, inquiry, and the IB Primary Years Programme (PYP). In this culturally diverse environment, students from over 50 nationalities learn to develop global perspectives and essential life skills such as communication and resilience. Located on a spacious 16-acre campus in Oberursel, surrounded by forests, the school provides modern facilities where students engage in a rich curriculum that includes mathematics, language arts, science, social studies, physical education, art, music, and German language classes. The school emphasizes safety and well-being, supported by a dedicated team of educators. FIS also offers a variety of extracurricular activities that enhance learning beyond the classroom and help children discover their talents. With its focus on whole-child education and a nurturing community, FIS Primary School is an ideal place for early childhood education.

description_en: The Lycée Français International Victor Hugo is a private international school located in the Praunheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. The primary school component of this institution uniquely integrates the French educational system with an international curriculum that meets rigorous German educational standards. Students learn both French and German from an early age, with two distinct programs ensuring proficiency in both languages. Starting in first grade, all students also begin English instruction, which is formally taught twice a week for ninety minutes from third grade onwards. Additionally, the school offers instruction in Spanish, Chinese, and Latin, providing students with a diverse linguistic foundation essential for their future academic and professional success. With approximately 940 students representing 35 different nationalities, the school fosters a genuinely international learning environment that promotes intercultural understanding and global perspectives. Enrollment is open to children from age three, regardless of nationality or prior French language skills. The school also provides all-day programming and extended care options to accommodate families' varied schedules. Headmistress Karima Kirouani and the dedicated bilingual staff are committed to ensuring that all students thrive in this bilingual setting.

description_en: Freie Christliche Schule Frankfurt (FCSF) is a state-recognized private Christian school located in the Fechenheim neighborhood of Frankfurt. The primary school division emphasizes a harmonious atmosphere where biblical values are integrated into daily life. Individualized support is a cornerstone of the educational approach, allowing students to thrive in a nurturing environment. The modern school building, operational since 2018, features inviting classrooms, collaborative spaces, and a self-learning center that encourages student independence. Instruction is primarily in German, with English introduced as the first foreign language, alongside offerings in French, Latin, and Spanish. Parents appreciate the warm community and respectful environment, making it a welcoming space for families from all denominations. For those seeking a holistic, values-based education, the FCSF stands out as an exceptional choice.

description_en: Freie Schule Frankfurt (FSF) is a state-approved private alternative school located in Frankfurt am Main, offering a unique, child-centered educational experience for children aged one to 13 since 1974. The school emphasizes self-regulated learning within a nurturing all-day environment that combines kindergarten, primary school, and transitional stages. With two locations in Sachsenhausen and Preungesheim, FSF fosters a close-knit, family-like atmosphere where children can explore their interests freely. The original site in Sachsenhausen accommodates around 50 children, while the modern facility in Preungesheim, opened in 2013, features child-friendly spaces such as an atelier and woodworking workshop. FSF operates on a distinctive pedagogical model based on self-regulation and democratic principles, allowing children to determine their own learning activities with the guidance of dedicated educators. The school offers various afternoon activities and language programs, including English, and values creativity, movement, and holistic child development. With reliable full-day care, FSF is an ideal choice for working families seeking individualized support and community-oriented learning.

description: Freie Waldorfschule ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Friedlebenstraße 52, 60433 Frankfurt am Main. Die Schule ist in privater Trägerschaft. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en: The Freie Waldorfschule Frankfurt is a state-recognized private Waldorf school located in the Dornbusch neighborhood, serving approximately 825 to 1,000 students from preschool through grade 13. Established in 1947, it is the largest Waldorf school in Germany, offering a holistic education based on the principles of Rudolf Steiner. The school's unique curriculum features an epoch-based teaching model, where students engage deeply with subjects like German, history, and mathematics for several weeks at a time. They also learn foreign languages, including English and French, from an early age. The school is committed to fostering creativity and practical skills, with modern facilities that include workshops for arts and crafts. Extracurricular activities, sports, and aftercare programs ensure a well-rounded experience for students. The Freie Waldorfschule promotes personal growth in a nurturing environment and welcomes all children, regardless of their background or family income. Parents are encouraged to explore the school through guided tours and informational events and to actively participate in the school community.
foerderverein: "Förderverein der Freien Waldorfschule Frankfurt e.V.", mail@waldorfschule-frankfurt.de
namensgebung: Der Name Waldorfschule hat seinen Ursprung von „Waldorf-Astoria". Emil Molt, Inhaber der Waldorf-Astoria-Zigarettenfabik, hatte 1919 die Idee, eine Schule für die Kinder „seiner" Arbeiter einzurichten. Er beauftragte Rudolf Steiner mit der Gründung dieser Schule. Im Zentrum der Bemühungen Steiners stand eine ganzheitliche Pädagogik, eine Pädagogik, durch die Leib, Seele und Geist gleichermaßen gefördert werden sollten.
poi_primary_school_02_address: Engelthaler Str. 34, 60435 Frankfurt am Main, Germany
tuition_granular_reasoning: The Freie Waldorfschule Frankfurt charges a standard monthly tuition (Regelbeitrag) of €326.50. As a Waldorf school, it offers income-based reductions (Ermäßigungsanträge) for families with lower financial means. Following standard guidelines for income-based schools, the estimated fee scale calculates tuition at approximately 3% of gross annual income, subject to a minimum cap of €120 and a maximum cap at the standard fee of €326.50. This creates a gradient where lower-income households pay the minimum, middle-income households pay a scaled amount, and higher-income households pay the full Regelbeitrag. Since specific sibling discount rates are not publicly published, conservative default values of 10% for the second child, 15% for the third, and 20% for the fourth and beyond have been applied.
tuition_tier_reasoning: According to the publicly available Beitragsordnung (fee schedule) for the Freie Waldorfschule Frankfurt, the standard monthly tuition fee (Regelbeitrag) for a single child is €326.50. While families can apply for income-based fee reductions through a committee, the baseline tuition determines the cost level. Excluding one-time admission and registration fees, the monthly rate falls strictly into the €301-€500 range, categorizing it as a 'high' tier.

description_en: Grundschule Riedberg is a public primary school located in the Kalbach-Riedberg neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. It serves children aged 6 to 10, providing a solid and welcoming learning environment that emphasizes foundational skills in reading, writing, mathematics, and social development. The school is dedicated to equal educational opportunities and follows a modern, hands-on teaching approach, ensuring all children in the district can attend without tuition fees. With an enrollment of 394 students, Grundschule Riedberg fosters a close-knit community where teachers can offer personalized attention. One of the school's standout features is its commitment to accessibility, with a fully wheelchair-friendly entrance that allows all children, including those with mobility needs, to participate fully in school life. Parents appreciate the school's transparent communication and inviting atmosphere, making it a reliable choice for their children's educational journey. Additionally, the school's location near other educational institutions enhances community ties. Overall, Grundschule Riedberg stands out as a dependable option for families seeking a nurturing, inclusive, and equitable primary education in Frankfurt am Main.

description_en: Gruneliusschule, affectionately known as "Gruni," is a public primary school located in the Oberrad neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. Serving approximately 402 students from grades 1 to 4, the school is staffed by around 45 dedicated teachers who create a supportive learning environment within a five-class structure. Nestled at the edge of the Stadtwald, it provides a picturesque setting for young learners. As an Open Frankfurt All-Day School since 2010, Gruneliusschule offers reliable care from 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., including breakfast and lunch in its onsite cafeteria. The school emphasizes inclusive education, allowing children of all abilities to learn together. Additionally, language support is available, with Turkish offered as an elective course. Music education is enhanced through a partnership with the Musikschule Frankfurt, providing various instrumental and ensemble activities right on campus. With its commitment to holistic education and cultural enrichment, Gruneliusschule fosters a vibrant and nurturing environment where children can thrive both academically and socially.

description_en: The Günderrode-Schule is a public primary school located in the Gallus district of Frankfurt am Main. As a two-stream school, it operates with two parallel classes per grade level, providing a structured environment for young learners aged 6 to 10 years. The school's central location in a diverse neighborhood makes it convenient for families. A recent expansion has added 4,300 square meters of modern facilities, including spacious classrooms, play areas, and specialized rooms, reflecting Frankfurt's commitment to quality public education. The school prioritizes a safe and nurturing environment tailored to primary-aged children, with a curriculum that includes core subjects such as German, mathematics, and general knowledge, as well as arts, sports, and social-emotional learning. Being a public institution, it is tuition-free, ensuring equal access for all families. With an enrollment of 388 students, the Günderrode-Schule is able to provide personalized attention while accommodating the growing community. Families interested in enrollment should contact the Stadtschulamt Frankfurt.

description_en: Heinrich-Kromer-Schule is a public primary school located in the Niederursel neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, catering to children from grades 1 to 4. With approximately 371 students across 18 regular classes and a preparatory class, the school fosters a nurturing environment tailored for young learners. It places a strong emphasis on individual support, offering all-day programs, special reading promotion, and enrichment for gifted students. A standout feature is the school's garden, which provides hands-on experiences in nature for the children. With over 90 years of history, the Heinrich-Kromer-Schule has continually evolved to meet the needs of its community. The dedicated teaching staff and support team create an optimal learning atmosphere. An active parents' council promotes collaboration between teachers and families through regular events. Looking ahead, the school is set for a comprehensive redevelopment to accommodate the growing population of Frankfurt and enhance its facilities.

description_en: Heinrich-Seliger-Schule is a public primary school located in the Dornbusch neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. Serving children aged 6 to 10, it provides a strong foundational education within a welcoming community environment, with an enrollment of 395 students for the 2024/25 school year. The school emphasizes a supportive learning atmosphere where students can thrive and showcases its commitment to community through engaging events, such as welcoming new first-graders with performances from older students. Additionally, Heinrich-Seliger-Schule is dedicated to enhancing accessibility, ensuring that all families, including those with disabilities, can easily access important information. The curriculum aligns with Hesse's state standards, covering core subjects like German, math, and general knowledge in a nurturing setting. Parents appreciate the school's proximity to green spaces and its family-friendly atmosphere, contributing to a safe daily routine for students. Overall, Heinrich-Seliger-Schule is an excellent choice for families seeking quality education without tuition fees, backed by a modern, inclusive philosophy.

The educational philosophy at Hellerhofschule focuses on openness and collaboration, allowing all students to explore their talents and interests. Strong partnerships with parents and the community are integral to fostering a supportive atmosphere for students. description_en: Henri-Dunant-Schule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, catering to students from grades 1 to 4. Housed in a modern building completed in 2020, the school emphasizes a welcoming, child-centered environment that promotes respectful and peaceful interactions among students. With approximately 339 students enrolled, Henri-Dunant-Schule embodies the principle of "school as a living space," offering not only standard classrooms but also dedicated areas for learning and leisure. A key feature of the school is its comprehensive all-day care program, which includes early morning supervision, extended school-based care, and an after-school club, all designed to foster independence and student participation. The school excels in art and music, providing creative development opportunities that appeal to families. With a dedicated team of educators and an active parent association, Henri-Dunant-Schule stands out as an excellent choice for families seeking a modern and inclusive primary education in Frankfurt.

description_en: Holzhausenschule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, serving approximately 484 students in the 2024/25 school year. Situated in the vibrant Nordend/Westend neighborhood, the school is easily accessible via the Miquelallee U-Bahn station, just 300 meters away. As a city-owned institution, Holzhausenschule emphasizes inclusivity, multilingualism, and creative development. A standout feature is its strong bilingual and trilingual profile, offering dedicated German-Italian classes for early language immersion, along with a supplementary Greek-language program. The school is also a certified Musical Primary School, integrating music into daily activities. Holzhausenschule prioritizes holistic child development through regular art projects and participates in the Pakt für den Ganztag, addressing the increasing demand for full-day care. The active parent-teacher association supports the school by funding projects and extracurricular activities, enhancing the sense of community. With its focus on respect, tolerance, and self-determination, Holzhausenschule is an excellent choice for families seeking a nurturing and inclusive educational environment.

description_en: Hostatoschule is a public primary and secondary school located in the Höchst neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. It provides an inclusive and welcoming learning environment for children and young people from diverse cultural backgrounds. With the motto “One World – Hostatoschule Höchst,” the school fosters respectful interactions among students and strengthens community ties through close cooperation with local businesses. Hostatoschule is committed to integration and offers a comprehensive all-day program that includes afternoon activities, meals, and holiday care alongside regular class hours. The school excels in enhancing German language skills and offers heritage language instruction in Turkish and Spanish, ensuring that students are well-prepared for their future. Under the leadership of Principal Marianna Papadopoulou, the school emphasizes a safe and inclusive atmosphere where cultural diversity is celebrated as an asset. It is ideal for families seeking a supportive environment with strong community connections, actively engaging in the growth and development of their children.

description: I. E.Lichtigfeld-Schule (Grundschule) ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Westendstraße 45-47, 60325 Frankfurt am Main. Die Schule ist in privater Trägerschaft. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en: The I. E. Lichtigfeld-Schule is a state-recognized private primary school operated by the Jewish Community of Frankfurt am Main, located in the Westend neighborhood. It offers a welcoming full-day educational environment that emphasizes individualized learning and small class sizes, with a maximum of 24 students per class. This allows for personalized attention from well-trained teachers. The school integrates Jewish values with modern pedagogy, fostering a love for learning. A distinctive feature is its participation in the 'Musikalische Grundschule' project, which enriches the curriculum with musical experiences that engage students and ignite their enthusiasm for learning. The school also offers a diverse language program, including English, Modern Hebrew, French, and Spanish, reflecting its commitment to multilingualism. With approximately 670 students, the school maintains a vibrant community while emphasizing academic success and personal growth. The I. E. Lichtigfeld-Schule is an excellent choice for families seeking a private education rooted in Jewish community values in Frankfurt.

description: ibms International Bilingual Montessori School ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Senckenberganlage 20-22, 60325 Frankfurt am Main. Die Schule ist in privater Trägerschaft. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en: The International Bilingual Montessori School (ibms) is a private, independent primary school in Frankfurt am Main, specializing in bilingual education based on Montessori principles for children aged 18 months to around 10 years. Established in 1996 as Frankfurt's first bilingual Montessori school, it offers a nurturing learning environment surrounded by urban greenery. The school's innovative approach blends international Montessori pedagogy with the German curriculum, allowing for individualized support tailored to each child's needs. Bilingualism is at the heart of daily life, with instruction in both German and English delivered by two teachers per class, fostering natural language acquisition from an early age. Programs include Nido for the youngest infants and the Children's House for preschoolers, which promote social skills and empathy among students. The school emphasizes the development of curious thinkers and responsible community members within a multicultural setting. With small class sizes and a dual-teacher model, students receive personalized attention that boosts their confidence and language fluency. Parents appreciate the balance between international principles and German educational expectations, ensuring a smooth transition for children into the local education system.
foerderverein: "Freunde und Förderer der IBMS e.V."

tuition_granular_reasoning: [GPT-5.2 Pass3] Source: IBMS Frankfurt official page 'Schulgebühren' (accessed 2026-04-07) lists Grundschule monthly fee €1,215 for the first child and €1,000 for the second & further children, including school fees, supervision/aftercare fees, and meal fee. It states the Grundschule fees are charged in 10 installments from August to May and a one-time admission fee of €2,500 per child. The page does not publish any income-based sliding scale; it only mentions a hardship rule ('Härtefallregelung') for lower-income families on request, without a schedule or income definition. Therefore tuition is treated as not income-based for computation; all provided income points map to the same standard monthly charge for the first child. Sibling discount computed as (1215-1000)/1215 ≈ 17.7%, applied from the 2nd child onward.
tuition_tier_reasoning: Based on the fee schedule for the International Bilingual Montessori School (ibms) in Frankfurt, fees are billed in 10 instalments per year. With a strong commitment to holistic education and a nurturing learning environment, IGS Eschersheim is an excellent choice for families.

description_en:
IKS - Interkulturelle Schule Rhein-Main is a private primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, known for its strong emphasis on cultural diversity and integration. With around 201 students, the school provides a nurturing environment for elementary-aged children, fostering intercultural understanding and dialogue among students from various backgrounds. The school’s educational philosophy celebrates diversity as a strength, offering students the chance to learn in a respectful and inclusive setting.

The curriculum is designed with flexibility to cater to the diverse needs of students, placing a strong emphasis on developing intercultural competencies. In addition to the core academic program, the school offers a variety of extracurricular activities that promote social engagement and personal growth.

IKS - Interkulturelle Schule Rhein-Main is proud to be a community that welcomes families and reflects the cultural diversity of Frankfurt. Parents interested in learning more about the school are encouraged to directly contact the administration for detailed information about specific programs, language offerings, and educational methods.

This categorizes the school in the 'medium' cost tier (€101-300). Extra charges, such as the €90 monthly fee for meals, are excluded from this calculation.

description_en: The ISF International School Frankfurt-Rhein-Main (Primary School) is the primary section of the ISF International School, located in the Sindlingen neighborhood of Frankfurt. Since its establishment in 1995, the school has evolved into a vibrant educational institution that welcomes students from around 50 nations on a spacious, state-of-the-art campus spanning 47,000 m². The primary program emphasizes a balanced approach to academic, social, physical, and emotional development, ensuring that children thrive during these formative years. The curriculum is anchored in the proven SABIS Educational System, with English as the primary language of instruction. Additionally, German is offered flexibly, catering to both native speakers and learners of various proficiency levels. The school also provides a range of other language options, including French, Spanish, and Japanese. Arts, music, and physical education play crucial roles, fostering well-rounded, enthusiastic learners. The school operates a full-day program with after-school care, offering organized activities such as sports and clubs. Parents appreciate the tailored support that helps their children succeed in this international environment. As an accredited institution of the Council of International Schools, ISF prepares students for successful academic futures.

description_en: The Japanische Internationale Schule Frankfurt (Grundschule) is located at Langweidenstraße 8-12 in the Hausen neighborhood and is a state-recognized private supplementary primary school. This institution follows the Japanese educational system, with instruction primarily in Japanese and German taught as a foreign language from the first to the ninth grades. Originally established as a Saturday school in the 1960s for children of Japanese business employees, the school has since grown into a comprehensive institution that caters to students up to grade six. A key focus of the school is language instruction, as students are immersed in a multilingual environment where they learn Japanese, German, and English. The campus features a spacious, well-equipped building that also houses an affiliated Japanese preschool, facilitating a smooth transition from early education to primary school. With an emphasis on a holistic educational experience, the school offers afternoon programs that promote social, creative, and physical development alongside academics. The Japanische Internationale Schule Frankfurt provides expatriate families with a unique opportunity for a culturally enriching and linguistically diverse primary education.

description: Judith-Kerr-Schule ist eine Grundschule in Frankfurt am Main. 7% der Schüler haben nichtdeutsche Herkunftssprache. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Mittleres Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (13/Jahr).
description_en: Judith-Kerr-Schule is a public primary school located in the Riedberg neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, serving students from grades 1 to 4. With an enrollment of approximately 185 students, the school provides a modern learning environment that has been steadily growing since its opening in August 2019. A standout feature of Judith-Kerr-Schule is its 'flexible school start' program, which enables teachers to work closely with social pedagogues in 'flex classes', fostering individualized support based on each child's learning level. In addition to regular school hours, the school offers comprehensive after-school care through its Extended School Care (ESB) and various extracurricular programs. These options allow children to participate in supervised activities and receive homework assistance. The school places a strong emphasis on social and emotional development and collaborates closely with Evangelische Jugendhilfe Frankfurt. Under the leadership of Principal Amrei Quandel, Judith-Kerr-Schule promotes a modern, child-centered pedagogy within a supportive public framework, making it particularly appealing for families seeking a well-rounded education for their children.
foerderverein: Freundeskreis der Judith-Kerr-Schule e.V.
namensgebung: Judith Kerr (1923-2019), Schriftstellerin
schulbibliothek: Von der Stadtbücherei aufgebaut und organisiert. Ausleihe durch die Eltern; Teilnahme an der "Kalbacher Klapperschlange".
schulform_bemerkung: Die Judith-Kerr-Schule ist eine Schule im Flexiblen Schulanfang („Flex“). Dabei sind die Jahrgangsstufen 1 und 2 zu einer pädagogischen Einheit zusammengefasst, der sogenannten „Flexklasse“. In den Flexklassen unterrichten Lehrerinnen und Lehrer stundenweise gemeinsam mit Sozialpädagoginnen und Sozialpädagogen. Dies ermöglicht eine noch lerngruppenspezifischere und individuellere Förderung des einzelnen Kindes. Die Schülerinnen und Schüler haben die Möglichkeit, entsprechend ihrem jeweiligen Lern- und Leistungsvermögen die zusammengefassten Jahrgangsstufen 1 und 2 in einem, in zwei oder in drei Schuljahren zu durchlaufen.

Situated at Am Spritzenhaus 2, it offers free education for children aged 6 to 10, covering grades 1 through 4. The school adheres to the Hessian curriculum, emphasizing holistic development through core subjects such as German, mathematics, and introductory English, along with arts, physical education, and science. A significant feature of Kerschensteinerschule is its participation in the Eco-School program, which promotes sustainable practices and incorporates environmental education into the curriculum. With 357 students enrolled, the school maintains a reasonable class size that supports individualized attention. The family-friendly Hausen area provides a supportive backdrop for education, complete with nearby parks and playgrounds that enhance school life. Parents can easily contact the school for inquiries or to arrange visits. Kerschensteinerschule stands out as a strong choice for families seeking a nurturing, environmentally conscious primary education.

description_en: Konrad-Haenisch-Schule is a public primary school located in the Fechenheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main. With approximately 590 students, the school provides a supportive environment within a multicultural community. A key strength of the school is its emphasis on inclusive education, complemented by specialized language programs in English, Arabic, and Turkish, as well as targeted support for German language acquisition. The school focuses on holistic student development, offering a comprehensive all-day program that includes extracurricular activities (AGs) and extended care services. Since 2010, the school has collaborated with the Internationaler Bund to provide tailored after-school programs, including holiday care with themed activities. The school also partners with youth welfare organizations to deliver preventive and intervention support for students and parents. Overall, it stands out as a nurturing and inclusive environment that combines academic foundations with enriching musical and social opportunities in a community-focused setting.

description_en: Main Schule is a private bilingual primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, offering full-day care and an immersive multilingual education program for young children. Integrated into the Hessian state education system, the school emphasizes small class sizes, personalized development, and extended daily support from 7:30 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., making it an appealing choice for families seeking a structured yet nurturing environment. A key feature of Main Schule is its innovative language learning approach, where children engage in one language each day. Starting from first grade, the curriculum incorporates three languages: German as the primary language, English for 6-7 hours per week, and Russian for 2 hours per week. This bilingual and trilingual focus fosters international mindedness through student exchange programs and offers opportunities for both teachers and staff. The afternoon program is diverse, including creative arts, music, sports, performing arts, additional language practice, and natural sciences, all designed to nurture individual talents. Daily operations blend academic instruction with full-day supervision, including homework assistance, ensuring balanced support for educational progress and personal growth in small-class settings. The school maintains high standards within the public education framework, and its selective enrollment process reflects a commitment to finding the right fit between students, families, and the school community. Families interested in learning more can contact the school for personalized guidance and upcoming events.

tuition_granular_reasoning: [GPT-5.2 Pass3] Used the school's official enrollment/cost information page (main-schule.de/aufnahme). The page states a single flat 'Monatliche Beiträge' amount of 610 EUR including lunch, and does not reference any income-based scale; therefore tuition is treated as not income-based and identical for all provided household gross annual income points. The school defines its financial school year as 01.08–31.07, implying 12 monthly billing months. The page lists additional mandatory upfront/annual items (application fee 50 EUR, deposit/loan 1000 EUR, school association membership 200 EUR/year, learning materials 240 EUR/year, and an admission/processing fee 300 EUR). No sibling discount is mentioned on the current official fee info, so sibling discounts are reported as 0% (not found).
tuition_tier_reasoning: Main Schule's website lists a total monthly fee of €610, which explicitly includes meals (Mittagessen) and full-day care (Ganztagsbetreuung). According to the official 2022 Hessen Landtag report on private school fees, the pure base tuition (Schulgeld) for Main Schule—which strictly excludes meals and aftercare—is €300 per month. Since the rules require excluding meals and aftercare, the tuition is €300/month, placing it exactly at the upper limit of the 'medium' tier (€101-300/month).

description_en: The Margarete-Steiff-Schule is a private inclusive primary school located in the Ginnheim neighborhood of Frankfurt am Main, serving children from grades 1 to 4. Since its founding in 1985, it has been one of Frankfurt's pioneering integrative schools, embracing a child-centered approach. With the motto "Strengthen strengths and weaken weaknesses," the school promotes a learning environment free from performance pressure and grades. Classes are held in bright, welcoming, and digitally equipped rooms designed to meet the diverse needs of students. Each of the eight classes accommodates up to 22 children, with approximately five receiving special pedagogical support from a multidisciplinary team of professionals. The school values social togetherness and emphasizes learning rooted in Christian values. With around 176 students in the 2024/25 school year, it fosters a close-knit community and offers holistic, inclusive education that seamlessly integrates regular and special education elements. Named after Margarete Steiff, the inventor of the teddy bear, the school symbolizes resilience and inclusion. Parents appreciate the nurturing environment where every child is welcomed, regardless of their abilities.

description_en: Metropolitan School Frankfurt (MSF) is a vibrant, family-oriented international primary school located in the Rödelheim district of Frankfurt, serving approximately 675 students from 55 nationalities. Founded in 2007, the school operates as a co-educational day school, with English as the primary language of instruction from Pre-Kindergarten through Grade 12. MSF follows a rigorous international curriculum, offering the International Baccalaureate Primary Years Programme (IB PYP), which prepares students with a global perspective. Additionally, the school emphasizes language education by teaching German, French, and Spanish, further enhancing students' linguistic skills. Known for its impressive academic achievements, MSF boasts a 100% pass rate for the IB program. The campus features over 8,000 square meters of well-designed space, including bright classrooms, modern laboratories, art studios, and the largest sports hall in Frankfurt. Beyond academics, MSF offers a wide range of extracurricular activities and afternoon programs that allow students to explore their interests and talents. The school fosters a warm, inclusive atmosphere where every student is valued, aiming to inspire self-confident and happy learners. MSF provides flexible tuition payment options and offers reduced fees for qualifying families, ensuring high-quality international education is accessible to all families.

description_en: Phorms Schule Frankfurt is a bilingual private primary school that offers immersive German-English education for children aged 5 to 10. With over 300 students across two campuses in the Frankfurt area, the City Campus is situated in the vibrant Holzhausenviertel. This beautifully modernized historic post office building combines tradition with innovative learning spaces, creating a serene environment. As part of the Phorms Education Network, which has promoted bilingual education for over 15 years, the school emphasizes curiosity-driven learning and future-oriented skills within an international community. The primary school serves as an entrance level through the fourth grade, featuring a structured all-day program from 8:45 a.m. to 3:45 p.m., including optional early morning supervision starting at 7:30 a.m. The curriculum is focused on bilingual immersion, with dedicated international teachers instructing in their native languages, helping students build fluency through engaging methods. Subjects include German, English, Math, General Studies, Sports, Art, Music, and Ethics. Phorms Frankfurt is known for its commitment to emotional strength and anti-bullying values, making it an ideal choice for families seeking a high-quality primary education that prepares children for a connected world.

description_en:
The Private Kant-Schule in Frankfurt am Main is a private, state-recognized replacement primary school with a rich history spanning over 60 years. Located at Hammanstraße 9, near Holzhausenpark, the school offers a dependable full-day program grounded in a humanistic educational philosophy. With a maximum capacity for 200 students, it emphasizes individualized support, traditional core skills, and a nurturing environment for children from grades 1 to 4, providing a seamless transition to its affiliated nine-year Gymnasium.

The school operates from 7:30 a.m. to 5:00 p.m., Monday through Friday, allowing ample time for academic instruction, homework assistance, and extracurricular activities. During school holidays, supervised vacation programs are available, featuring theater visits and excursions to engaging locations around Frankfurt. The curriculum prioritizes foundational skills such as writing, reading, and arithmetic, while foreign languages, including English, French, and Latin, are introduced from grade 5 onward.

Monthly tuition is 420 euros, covering full-day care, meals, and all necessary teaching materials, making it an all-inclusive model that supports working families. Parents appreciate the emphasis on holistic development in a secure and engaging environment. The Private Kant-Schule stands out as a trusted choice for families seeking a stable and comprehensive primary education in Frankfurt.

This fee is a mandatory, all-inclusive 'Rundumversorgung' that covers the core tuition, full-day care, daily meals (Verpflegung), and all learning materials. While the prompt requests excluding meals and aftercare, the school's flat-rate structure does not publicly itemize or allow opting out of these costs. This securely places the school in the 'high' cost tier (€301-500/month).

description_en: Riedhofschule is a public primary school located in the Sachsenhausen district of Frankfurt am Main, catering to children from preschool through fourth grade. Nestled in the family-friendly Heimatsiedlung, which was built on the former estate grounds of the 'Riedhof' manor, the school emphasizes inclusive education and community integration under the leadership of Principal Martina Ruppel. With approximately 393 students enrolled across 16 classes and a preschool class, Riedhofschule provides a vibrant and supportive learning environment. Notably, the school offers preparatory language courses for children with German as a second language, helping them smoothly transition into formal schooling. Additionally, the school participates in the 'Youth Services in Primary School' program, promoting social and emotional development within an inclusive framework. The traditional enrollment celebration fosters a warm community atmosphere, welcoming new students and their families. A structured daily schedule extends until 5:00 PM, accommodating working parents and after-school activities. With its location providing easy access to green spaces and local amenities, Riedhofschule stands out as an excellent choice for families seeking a nurturing environment for their primary-aged children.

description_en: The Robert-Blum-Schule is a public primary school located in the vibrant Höchst district of Frankfurt am Main, serving grades 1 through 4. With approximately 475 students, the school offers a nurturing environment that allows for personalized attention. It is well-regarded for its diverse educational programs, particularly for supporting children with different backgrounds through specialized pre-classes and language preparatory courses. As a full-day school, Robert-Blum-Schule provides comprehensive care that combines learning with extracurricular activities, fostering the holistic development of children. The teaching staff collaborates closely with external caregivers to ensure high-quality after-school programming. Celebrating its 100th anniversary in 2008, the school has deep roots in the local community. Parents appreciate the variety of extracurricular activities that enrich the educational experience and allow children to explore their interests. Robert-Blum-Schule stands out as an excellent choice for families seeking a supportive and inclusive learning environment.

description_en: The Robert-Schumann-Schule is a public primary school located in Frankfurt am Main, Germany, catering to children in grades 1 through 4 (ages 6 to 10). As a city-owned institution, it provides free education to local families, focusing on foundational skills in reading, writing, mathematics, and social development within a nurturing environment. With an enrollment of 377 students for the 2024/25 school year, the school fosters a close-knit community that allows for personalized attention from teachers. The Robert-Schumann-Schule actively supports students in their transition to secondary education, offering comprehensive information about recommendations and next steps tailored to each child's progress. Situated in a culturally rich neighborhood, the school likely promotes an appreciation for arts and music as part of its curriculum. It adheres to Hesse's state educational standards, delivering a balanced curriculum that includes German language arts, math, general studies, arts, physical education, and religion or ethics. Parents appreciate the school’s public status, which eliminates tuition fees and ensures high-quality facilities funded by the city. The Robert-Schumann-Schule is a dependable choice for families seeking a supportive, community-oriented primary education. Prospective parents are encouraged to contact the school during office hours to learn more about enrollment or arrange a visit.

With approximately 196 students enrolled, the school offers a nurturing environment and smaller class sizes that allow for personalized attention. A standout feature is its all-day program, which extends beyond traditional school hours, providing structured care, meals, and activities that support working families. This setup ensures that children not only receive a quality education but also have the opportunity to develop socially in a safe space. The school emphasizes foundational skills in reading, writing, and mathematics while integrating support for homework and play-based learning. For families in Frankfurt seeking a reliable and supportive educational institution, Schule am Erlenbach stands out as an excellent choice.

With an enrollment of 463 students for the 2024/25 school year, the school provides a nurturing environment that emphasizes essential skills in reading, writing, and arithmetic while also fostering social competencies. Its ideal location in a family-friendly neighborhood, close to attractions like the Frankfurt Zoo and the Botanical Garden, offers students ample opportunities for outdoor learning and extracurricular activities. Schule am Hang is dedicated to building strong ties within the community and provides parents with comprehensive resources through its official website, ensuring transparency and accessibility. The school also prioritizes health and safety with established hygiene protocols. Overall, Schule am Hang stands out as a reliable choice for families seeking quality early education in Frankfurt.
